headline: OpenAI reports Chinese influence operations targeting US AI policy debates
body: OpenAI disclosed that China-linked accounts are conducting coordinated influence campaigns aimed at shaping US policy discussions around AI infrastructure and tariffs. The activity represents a geopolitical escalation in how foreign actors are engaging with AI governance decisions. For healthcare AI stakeholders, this matters because US regulatory frameworks and data residency policies directly impact where clinical AI systems can be deployed, how patient data flows across borders, and which vendors gain market access. Healthcare organizations and consultants need to monitor how these geopolitical tensions influence compliance requirements, cloud infrastructure choices, and the vendor consolidation happening around large language models used in clinical settings.
